error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to AiDot servers.

data.sdes_fast_liveplay: SDES fast connect (faster signaling, on by default)
data.sdes_adaptive: SDES adaptive fast-with-fallback (experimental, off by default)

data_description.serve_port_base: Base local port for serving SDES camera streams to HA/go2rtc (each camera uses base..base+399). Change only if it conflicts with another service.
data_description.connection_mode: How cameras connect. Relay (recommended) keeps the cloud relay available and lets the connection pick the best path - this is what the official app does, so it works on every network: same-LAN, a separate camera VLAN, or remote/strict-NAT. LAN-direct skips the relay for a faster (~1s vs ~3-4s) start, but a camera that is NOT on the same network as Home Assistant will fail to connect - choose it only if all cameras share the HA LAN and you want the quicker start.
data_description.sdes_audio: Include audio in SDES camera streams (PCMA transcoded to AAC). Disabled by default because battery and weak-signal cameras send audio too sparsely, which stalls the stream. Enable only for mains-powered cameras with a reliable connection.
data_description.sdes_fast_liveplay: Matches the official app: skip the livePlayResp wait and go straight to the video handshake, so SDES (battery) cameras start ~4.5 s sooner. ON by default. The PTZ camera is automatically excluded (its handshake needs the slower path). If you ever see a live view drop to a snapshot on a specific camera, turn this off.
data_description.sdes_adaptive: Tries the fast SDES connect first and automatically falls back to the reliable relay path if it doesn't deliver video, so a fast connect is safe even for cameras that aren't on the local network. A camera that fails the fast path is remembered and skips it on later views. Off by default while its real-world reliability is measured: a fast failure adds a delay (~40s) before the fallback, so leave it off unless you want to help test it.
data_description.mains_idle_s: How long to keep a mains-powered camera's live session warm after the last viewer, so re-opening it is instant instead of paying the full cold-start handshake. Default 120s. Set 0 to never release (always warm). Each warm camera uses a stream slot and keeps decrypting, so keep the total within the concurrent-stream cap (default 3). Battery cameras ignore this (they warm on motion to save battery).
data_description.enable_local_control: Route camera settings (LED, motion detection, night vision, sensitivity, volume, PTZ tracking) over the LAN instead of the cloud, for eligible mains-powered cameras. Faster and works during cloud outages; falls back to cloud automatically. Does not affect video. Battery cameras are skipped.

talk_no_audio.message: No audio could be decoded from '{media}' - check the media URL or path.

talk_session_failed.message: Could not establish a talk session (camera must be streaming - open the live view first).

ptz_failed.message: PTZ command '{direction}' was not sent (camera must be streaming - open the live view first).

command_rejected.message: AiDot {action} was rejected by the device.
